(宝塚記念) is a [Grade I](/wiki/Conditions_races "Conditions races") [flat](/wiki/Flat_racing "Flat racing") [horse race](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") in [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan") for [Thoroughbreds](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") aged three and over a distance of 2,200 metres (approximately 1+3⁄8 miles) at [Hanshin Racecourse](/wiki/Hanshin_Racecourse "Hanshin Racecourse") (阪神競馬場, *Hanshin Keibajō*) in late June. It is one of the two "All-Star" races in Japanese horse racing; the other being the [Arima Kinen](/wiki/Arima_Kinen "Arima Kinen") (the Grand Prix) held in late December.
* 2:09.7 – [Titleholder](/wiki/Titleholder_(horse) "Titleholder (horse)") (2022)
* 2 – [Chrono Genesis](/wiki/Chrono_Genesis "Chrono Genesis") (2020, 2021)
* 5 – [Yutaka Take](/wiki/Yutaka_Take "Yutaka Take") (1989, 1993, 1997, 2006, 2025)

The **Takarazuka Kinen** (宝塚記念) is a [Grade I](/wiki/Conditions_races "Conditions races") [flat](/wiki/Flat_racing "Flat racing") [horse race](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") in [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan") for [Thoroughbreds](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") aged three and over a distance of 2,200 metres (approximately 1+3⁄8 miles) at [Hanshin Racecourse](/wiki/Hanshin_Racecourse "Hanshin Racecourse") (阪神競馬場, *Hanshin Keibajō*) in late June. It is one of the two "All-Star" races in Japanese horse racing; the other being the [Arima Kinen](/wiki/Arima_Kinen "Arima Kinen") (the Grand Prix) held in late December.

The Takarazuka Kinen was first run in 1960 with a distance of 1,800 metres. From 1961 to 1965 the race was run over 2,000 metres and since 1966 it has been run over its present distance.

The race is run on the turf and is named after the city of [Takarazuka](/wiki/Takarazuka,_Hy%C5%8Dgo "Takarazuka, Hyōgo"), [Hyōgo](/wiki/Hy%C5%8Dgo_Prefecture "Hyōgo Prefecture"), the location of Hanshin Racecourse, which is the venue of the race.

As with the Arima Kinen, the majority of the runners in the field are selected by a vote from racing fans, while the remainder are determined by the amount of prize money won.
